goal: Remove every always-on service from `surface` so the stack survives the laptop being off. NO always-on local LLM floor: always-on inference = clouds (free→paid). A capable local floor returns later on a planned 64GB+ unified-memory node. ALL always-on local work (gateway, labwatch, STT, ingest) consolidates on the single M4 — with no local model it fits 16GB easily; both M2s are SOLD. Public on Hetzner.

decision_log.dropped_8_14b_floor: An 8-14B local model is far weaker than the free clouds (Cerebras 235B, Groq 70B) and only ever served as a mediocre emergency backstop. Removed. Failure backstop is now paid cloud, not free-local.
decision_log.accepted_tradeoff: Weakens SOVRN principle #1 (100% offline) to 'offline only when Surface 30B is up' — until the 64GB node lands and restores a proper always-on local floor.
decision_log.future_floor: Always-on local inference returns on a planned 64GB+ Apple-Silicon node running 30B-A3B / 70B-Q4 — see fleet.json planned-64gb-node.
decision_log.variant_a_consolidation: With no local LLM on the M4, STT + KG ingest fit alongside gateway+labwatch in 16GB (~6.5GB steady, ~9GB peak). So consolidate ALL always-on local work on the M4 and SELL both M2s (~$560). Accepted cost: M4 is a single point of failure for the local tier — tolerable because the inference backstop is cloud anyway, and the future 64GB node adds a second always-on box.

workloads.why: Critical control-plane piece — everything routes through it. M4 hosts it with huge headroom (no local model to share RAM with). Keeps provider API keys on the LAN. This is the gateway's permanent home — it does NOT move again when the 64GB node arrives (that box is pure inference).
workloads.notes: Lightweight Python proxy. Fallback chain must end at HF/paid, NOT local-fallback (no floor in interim). Port dynamic via port_broker → services.json.

workloads.why: Co-locate with the gateway (reads usage.db written by the LiteLLM callback).
workloads.notes: stdlib Python, 127.0.0.1. LAN-expose or SSH-tunnel to view from the workstation.

workloads.host_target: NONE in interim → planned 64GB node later
workloads.why: DROPPED as an always-on service. Interim: clouds carry inference (free→paid). The floor returns only when the 64GB node lands — running a capable 30B-A3B/70B, not a weak 8B.
workloads.notes: litellm-config: remove local-fallback from default_fallbacks; repoint chain tails to hf-llama-70b/paid.

workloads.why: Stays as the on-demand local/private/offline option WHEN the laptop is on. More important now there's no always-on floor — it's the only local inference until the 64GB box arrives.
workloads.notes: In LiteLLM rotation only while Surface is up; router skips it when down.

workloads.why: If it must answer 24/7 → M4 (co-located with gateway); if only during work sessions → on-demand.
workloads.notes: Open decision. Brain = gemini-3-flash-preview via gateway.

workloads.why: Genuinely-local workload (audio). Parakeet is tiny (~0.6B); with no local LLM on the M4 it fits alongside the gateway easily (~1-2GB during transcription).
workloads.notes: Echo transcription path calls the M4. (Consolidated — was M2 in the distributed variant.)

workloads.host_target: m4-16gb — but blocked on vault location
workloads.why: Ingest is light (~0.5GB) → co-locate on the M4. BUT the source vault lives on the laptop (see vault question).
workloads.notes: Phase 4: vault-on-laptop is the independence blocker. If Sprint 2 runs jina-v4 embeddings LOCALLY on the M4 (+4-8GB), re-check M4 headroom — that's the one case that might argue for keeping an M2.

workloads.host_target: neo4j → m4-16gb OR hetzner(upgraded); app → on-demand
workloads.why: If the graph must be queryable 24/7, Neo4j leaves the laptop. M4 has RAM headroom (no local model now). CX22 4GB does not.
workloads.notes: Sprint 2 jina-v4 re-embed (768→1024) is the natural relocation moment.

workloads.why: Hetzner already runs prod n8n + Langfuse v2. Remove the redundant local copies; point LiteLLM tracing at logs.synergify.com.
workloads.notes: Memory already flagged local Langfuse stack for teardown.

workloads.why: Move only if actually used 24/7; otherwise on-demand or drop.
workloads.notes: Don't migrate what you don't use.

workloads.why: Legacy convenience; no local floor to serve. Retire.
Already correctly in the cloud tier.
workloads.notes: Public ingress via CF tunnel ENERV-HUB.
